title: ferrotorch-core: irfft CPU pad/truncate semantics differ from PyTorch when half_n != output_n/2+1 — surfaced by phase 2.7 (#769)
description:
Fixture cpu_irfft len_8_pad_to_16: input shape [2, 9, 2] (rfft of len-16 padded), output_n=8.
PyTorch's irfft(spec_of_len_9, n=8) takes the first 5 (=8/2+1) elements of the spectrum and Hermitian-extends. ferrotorch's CPU path takes the first min(half_n, output_n)=8 elements of the spectrum (which are not Hermitian) and IFFTs that, producing wrong result.
Code: ferrotorch-core/src/fft.rs:419-444.
Surfaced by phase 2.7. cascade_skip applied for tag len_8_pad_to_16.
